title: Sacramento, CA
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The bill introduced by Assemblymember Bauer-Kahan will regulate automated decision tools (ADTs) that make consequential decisions.",
    "This is the first bill of its kind in the state aimed at regulating ADTs.",
    "Developers and users are required to mitigate and assess automated decision tools (ADTs) under the new bill.",
    "The use of decision-making via algorithms is becoming more prevalent in daily life.",
    "Automated decision tools are often trained on biased data, which can result in harm to marginalized communities.",
    "A study published in Science showed that a clinical algorithm was racially biased against Black patients in patient care.",
    "Increasing use of algorithms without regulations could erode the legitimacy of institutions and harm marginalized groups.",
    "AB 331 requires an impact assessment to be conducted and recorded by developers and users of ADTs.",
    "The data reported under AB 331 must include an analysis of potential adverse impact based on various protected classifications.",
    "Unchecked use of ADTs could lead to significant decisions being made without individuals' knowledge or consent.",
    "The unchecked use of ADTs could further engrain social injustice into institutions.",
    "Automated decision tools (ADT) are rapidly evolving."
  ]
}
```
